# Script Safety Checklist: Repository Foundation for CI/CD Pipeline

**Purpose**: Validate that script safety requirements are complete, clear, and consistent for sync-marketplace-versions.sh and all shell scripts in this feature
**Created**: 2026-03-24
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are error handling requirements defined for when marketplace.json is missing or unreadable? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK002 - Are error handling requirements defined for when marketplace.json contains malformed/invalid JSON? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK003 - Are error handling requirements defined for when plugin.json is missing? [Completeness, Spec FR-008]
- [x] CHK004 - Are error handling requirements defined for when plugin.json contains malformed/invalid JSON? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases] -- Fixed: Added "Malformed plugin.json" edge case to spec.md
- [x] CHK005 - Are error handling requirements defined for when plugin.json exists but has no version field? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK006 - Are requirements specified for handling a marketplace.json plugins array entry that has no source field? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases] -- Fixed: Added "Missing source field" edge case to spec.md
- [x] CHK007 - Are requirements specified for handling a marketplace.json plugins array entry that has no version field? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases] -- Fixed: Added "Missing version field in marketplace entry" edge case to spec.md
- [x] CHK008 - Is the requirement for jq dependency checking at startup explicitly defined with the expected error message content? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK009 - Are requirements defined for what happens when the jq write-back to marketplace.json fails (e.g., disk full, permission denied)? [Completeness, Plan] -- Fixed: Added "jq Write-Back Failure Handling" section to plan.md explaining set -e coverage

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK010 - Is "descriptive error message" quantified or specified with required content elements for each failure mode (FR-008, Edge Cases)? [Clarity, Spec FR-008] -- Fixed: FR-008 now requires error message to identify which plugin's file caused the failure
- [x] CHK011 - Is "non-zero exit code" specified with a concrete exit code value or strategy (e.g., exit 1 for all errors, or distinct codes per failure type)? [Clarity, Spec FR-008]
- [x] CHK012 - Is "unreadable" (in FR-008 "missing or unreadable") defined -- does it mean file permission denied, corrupted JSON, empty file, or all of these? [Clarity, Spec FR-008] -- Fixed: FR-008 now defines "unreadable" as file permissions or invalid JSON
- [x] CHK013 - Is the relative path resolution logic for the source field explicitly specified (e.g., how ./speckit-pro resolves to speckit-pro/.claude-plugin/plugin.json)? [Clarity, Spec FR-005]
- [x] CHK014 - Is the definition of "non-relative source" explicitly specified -- what source patterns are considered non-relative (e.g., GitHub URLs, git:// URIs, bare names)? [Clarity, Spec FR-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK015 - Are script safety requirements in the spec (FR-007: set -euo pipefail) consistent with Constitution Principle II (set -euo pipefail, no unquoted variables, no unchecked command results)? [Consistency, Spec FR-007] -- Fixed: FR-007 now explicitly references Constitution Principle II with all requirements
- [x] CHK016 - Does the spec explicitly require "no unquoted variables" and "no unchecked command results" for the sync script, or does it only reference the shebang and set -euo pipefail from FR-007? [Consistency, Spec FR-007] -- Fixed: FR-007 expanded to include "no unquoted variables, no unchecked command results"
- [x] CHK017 - Are the error handling requirements for malformed JSON consistent between the Edge Cases section ("jq will fail and set -e will cause the script to exit") and FR-008 ("exit with non-zero exit code and descriptive error message")? [Consistency, Spec Edge Cases vs FR-008]

## Idempotency Requirements

- [x] CHK018 - Is the idempotency requirement (FR-009) specified with a measurable criterion for "no modification" -- does it mean no file write at all, or write with identical content is acceptable? [Clarity, Spec FR-009] -- Fixed: FR-009 now specifies "file MUST NOT be written at all" with git status rationale
- [x] CHK019 - Are requirements defined for idempotent behavior when marketplace.json has trailing whitespace or formatting differences but equivalent JSON content? [Clarity, Plan] -- Fixed: Plan "Idempotency Implementation" section specifies comparison-before-write approach

## Variable Quoting and Shell Safety

- [x] CHK020 - Does the spec or plan explicitly require that all variables in the sync script are quoted to prevent word splitting and globbing? [Completeness, Spec FR-007 + Plan] -- Fixed: FR-007 now includes "no unquoted variables"; Plan adds "Script Safety" section with quoting details
- [x] CHK021 - Does the spec or plan require that all jq filter inputs are validated before processing (e.g., checking that the plugins array exists and is non-empty)? [Completeness, Plan] -- Fixed: Plan "jq Filter Validation Strategy" section added
- [x] CHK022 - Are requirements defined for handling file paths with spaces or special characters in plugin directory names? [Completeness, Plan] -- Fixed: Plan "File Path Handling" section added

## jq Error Handling

- [x] CHK023 - Are requirements defined for how jq filters should handle missing fields in marketplace.json entries (e.g., entry without a name or source field)? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases + Plan] -- Fixed: "Missing source field" edge case in spec + jq -e validation in plan
- [x] CHK024 - Are requirements defined for jq filter behavior when the plugins array in marketplace.json is empty? [Completeness, Plan] -- Fixed: Plan specifies empty array exits successfully with informational message
- [x] CHK025 - Does the spec address what happens when jq produces an unexpected null result from a filter (e.g., jsonpath returns null for version)? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases + Plan] -- Fixed: Spec "Missing version field" edge case + Plan jq -e flag requirement

## Test Coverage for Script Safety

- [x] CHK026 - Are test requirements (FR-012) specified for malformed JSON input scenarios (invalid marketplace.json, invalid plugin.json)? [Completeness, Spec FR-012] -- Fixed: FR-012 expanded
- [x] CHK027 - Are test requirements specified for the jq dependency check failure scenario? [Completeness, Spec FR-012] -- Fixed: FR-012 expanded
- [x] CHK028 - Are test requirements specified for the wrong-working-directory detection scenario? [Completeness, Spec FR-012] -- Fixed: FR-012 expanded
- [x] CHK029 - Are test requirements specified for the non-relative source skipping behavior? [Completeness, Spec FR-012] -- Fixed: FR-012 expanded
- [x] CHK030 - Are test requirements specified for the missing version field in plugin.json scenario? [Completeness, Spec FR-012] -- Fixed: FR-012 expanded

## Script Execution Safety

- [x] CHK031 - Does the spec require that the sync script uses a temporary file for atomic writes to marketplace.json (preventing corruption on interrupted writes)? [Completeness, Plan] -- Addressed: Plan "Atomic Write Decision" section explicitly documents why atomic writes are not required (YAGNI, git-tracked file)
- [x] CHK032 - Does the spec or plan address what happens if the sync script is interrupted mid-execution (e.g., Ctrl-C between reading plugin.json and writing marketplace.json)? [Completeness, Plan] -- Addressed: Plan "Atomic Write Decision" section documents git recovery path
- [x] CHK033 - Is the chmod +x requirement for the sync script explicitly stated in the spec or plan? [Completeness, Spec FR-007] -- Fixed: FR-007 now includes "the script file MUST be executable (chmod +x)"

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- All 20 [Gap] markers have been remediated in spec.md and/or plan.md
